w:r.w:t: Our implementation leverages OpenAI's capabilities through a two-agent system designed to combine visual verification with web-based fact-checking.
: Visual data collection and interaction with the Arcade platform.
Authenticates to the Arcade platform as a registered user
Navigates to individual game submission pages
Captures screenshots of games as they render on the platform
Extracts visible metadata (title, description, developer name, category)
Passes collected data to the Web Search Agent
Receives game screenshots, title, and description from User Agent
Uses OpenAI's web search tool to verify submitted information
Cross-references developer names, game titles, and claimed attributes
Applies evaluation metrics to score submission authenticity
OpenAI Responses API with web search tool enabled (

: The User Agent passes both the screenshot (as image input) and extracted text metadata to the Web Search Agent. The Web Search Agent's vision model analyzes the screenshot to understand the visual presentation, then uses that understanding along with the text metadata to formulate web search queries for verification.

To ensure submitted game information is trustworthy, the AI goes beyond reading metadata in isolation. It actively uses search and fetch tools to cross-reference key fields against publicly available data, and applies evaluation metrics to score each submission before producing a recommendation.

: The AI queries external sources using OpenAI's web search tool to confirm whether a developer, studio, or game title exists and matches the submitted description. The tool accepts text queries generated from both the extracted metadata and visual analysis of the game's presentation.

: When the web search tool returns relevant URLs, the AI can retrieve full web pages or data sources to validate specific claims in the game's description, such as genre, platform, or publisher details.

: Screenshots captured by the User Agent are analyzed to verify consistency between what the game claims to be (in metadata) and what it visually presents. This helps detect mismatched categories or misleading descriptions.

: Each submission is scored across multiple dimensions before a recommendation is produced. If data cannot be verified or scores fall below threshold, the AI flags the inconsistency in its reasoning and adjusts its recommendation accordingly.
Does the game title match known records or appear fabricated?
Is the developer name a real or recognizable entity?
Is the description sufficiently detailed and consistent with the category?
Does the described content align with the claimed genre/category?
Are all submitted fields internally consistent with each other?
Does the screenshot match the claimed game type and description?

Since we are using OpenAI's API key, all agent calls will route through OpenAI's Responses API with the web search tool enabled. The multimodal capability allows us to pass screenshots alongside text in a single API request.
If the User Agent fails to capture a screenshot, the Web Search Agent proceeds with text-only verification
If web search returns no results for a claimed game/developer, this is flagged as a red flag in the evaluation metrics
Network timeouts and API rate limits are handled with exponential backoff
The User Agent operates with dedicated credentials separate from admin accounts
Screenshots are processed in-memory and not persistently stored
All verification queries are logged for audit purposes
User Agent operates on a queue system to process submissions sequentially
Web Search Agent batches metadata extraction to minimize API calls
Caching mechanism for frequently verified developers/studios
